description: Merkle list-commitment algorithm registry. Each identifier names the tree construction used to compute the `root` of a `merkle[]` commitment, which lets one record commit to many content digests under a single on-chain root. The pinned `root_length_bytes` is the exact byte length the structural validator requires for the root under that identifier; any other length is a HASH_DIGEST_LENGTH_MISMATCH, and any unregistered identifier is an UNSUPPORTED_MERKLE_COMMIT_ALG. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors.

entries.description: Binary Merkle tree per the RFC 9162 Merkle Tree Hash, using SHA-256. Domain-separation prefixes are applied before hashing: a 0x00 byte prefixes each leaf input and a 0x01 byte prefixes each internal-node input, which prevents second-preimage attacks that swap a leaf for an internal node. The root is the SHA-256 output of the tree.
entries.reference: RFC 9162 §2.1.1 (Certificate Transparency v2.0, Merkle Tree definition)
